mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon as higher borrowing costs reduce corporate profit margins and equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, it signals a sudden deterioration in labor market tightness that triggers immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in imminent corporate earnings downgrades and recession risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold crossing fails to generate a statistically significant negative alpha (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer demand that forces corporate revenue revisions downward, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The joint decline in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or single-series effects dominate the predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a sustained drop in the index of industrial production reflects capacity utilization exhaustion and supply chain bottlenecks that erode corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The negative predictive relationship reverses or becomes statistically insignificant (Rank IC < 0.02) during late-cycle expansion periods, or 60-day forward returns remain positive despite industrial production declines.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index readings force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which immediately compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real wealth effects over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns during CPI accelerations are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent on a quarterly basis, it signals a severe demand shock that triggers broad equity deleveraging and negative forward returns as investors anticipate recessionary earnings cycles.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the GDP threshold crossing fails to generate a statistically significant negative alpha (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where nonfarm payrolls decline while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously confirms a structural labor market deterioration that suppresses consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint labor market deterioration fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or single-series effects dominate the predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, federal funds rates exceeding 4.5 percent trigger a liquidity drain that disproportionately impacts growth stocks, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: The negative predictive relationship reverses or becomes statistically insignificant (Rank IC < 0.02) during monetary tightening cycles, or 20-day forward returns remain positive despite rates above 4.5%.
